name: omni-safe-refactor
description: End-to-end safe refactor pipeline: understand the code, scope the blast radius, recall prior lessons, render a diff, validate, apply, then keep a rollback hook. Never let an LLM write to disk without review.
when_to_use: You have a concrete file/symbol to change and you want the full safety net: pre-edit understanding, blast-radius check, lint/type review, memory advisory, preview-validate-apply, and a rollback path on regret.
safety_notes: omni_skill never auto-executes these steps — the AI editor must call each tool itself.
Every step that hits disk is the AI's explicit decision; omni_skill only reads manifests.
patch.v2 path guard rejects absolute paths and any '..' segment before any backend write. Files must stay under the workspace root.
patch.v2 apply has an internal validate gate (apply_validate_gate). Validation cannot be bypassed silently — the explicit validate step is an *advance look* for AI/editor readability.
force=True is an explicit escape hatch. Only use it with user approval AND a force_reason describing why. The response will carry validation_bypassed=true so the bypass is auditable.
Do not rollback sessions marked unsafe_legacy_session=true; those are pre-r7 artefacts whose file paths now sit outside the workspace and must be inspected manually.
Step 10 (rollback) is conditional but must remain visible so the agent always has an undo path.

inputs.name: file
inputs.description: Workspace-relative path only. patch.v2 path guard rejects absolute paths and any '..' segment; supplying e.g. '/etc/x' or '../../outside.py' returns ok=false without writing to disk.

inputs.name: symbol
inputs.description: Symbol you're refactoring (function/class/method). Optional but enables impact + references + memory_advisory steps.

inputs.name: task
inputs.description: One-line task description for the context composer and the memory advisory query.

inputs.name: content
inputs.description: Full new content for the file (omni_patch does a full-file replacement).

inputs.name: session_id
inputs.description: Set after the apply step from the apply response; needed only if the rollback step runs.

steps.title: Gather minimum-necessary context
steps.purpose: Pull outline + diagnostics + recent git changes + memory hits in one call so the agent isn't editing blind. context.v2 also surfaces symbol_resolution and confidence so you can verify the symbol exists before proceeding.

failure_policy.notes: Without context, the rest of the recipe is editing blind.

steps.title: Check the blast radius
steps.purpose: Risk badge + callers + callees + suggested tests. impact.v2 returns risk='unknown' with confidence='low' when the symbol isn't in the call graph — treat that as a stop signal and re-confirm the name via omni_search(mode='symbol').

failure_policy.notes: Without a blast-radius read the patch can silently break N callers.

steps.title: Read pre-existing diagnostics
steps.purpose: Distinguish issues we'll inherit from issues introduced by the patch. Re-run after apply to confirm no new errors.

failure_policy.notes: Need a baseline to compare against post-apply.

steps.title: Find every callsite of the symbol (line-level)
steps.purpose: omni_impact returns graph-level callers/callees. Use omni_search(mode='references') for line-level and textual references — including tests, fixtures, string-literal mentions, and dynamic-dispatch sites the static graph misses. Strongly recommended for renames.

failure_policy.notes: References is best-effort; renames without it are riskier but not blocked.

steps.title: Read the symbol or range we're rewriting
steps.purpose: Confirm the AI is rewriting the right body and not a stale version. mode=symbol when a symbol is given, mode=outline otherwise.

failure_policy.notes: If we can't read the current body, content drafting is unsafe.

steps.title: Recall prior lessons about this symbol/file
steps.purpose: Use memory.v2 advisory to retrieve structured action_items, risks, and referenced_memories from past mistakes or solutions related to this symbol/file/task. Review action_items BEFORE drafting content — it is the cheapest way to avoid repeating a documented mistake.

failure_policy.notes: Memory is advisory and should not block a safe edit.

steps.title: Render a unified diff
steps.purpose: Show the user what would change before any byte hits disk. Triggers the patch.v2 workspace path guard — absolute paths and '..' segments are rejected here without any backend call.

failure_policy.notes: If preview fails (path-guard or backend error) the apply path won't recover.

steps.title: Run static checks on the proposed content (advance look)
steps.purpose: patch.v2 apply re-runs validation internally before writing (apply_validate_gate). This explicit validate step is an *advance check* for AI/editor readability — it surfaces structured checks[], counts, and tools_run so the agent can fix content before stepping on the gate. Apply will still block writes when validation_passed=false unless force=True is explicitly provided with force_reason.

failure_policy.notes: If validate errored (not just failed) the gate state is unknown — do not proceed.

steps.title: Write to disk + create snapshot + record EditSession
steps.purpose: Performs the actual write. patch.v2 apply re-runs validate internally (apply_validate_gate); when validation_passed=false the call returns ok=false and nothing hits disk. Apply takes a snapshot and returns session_id so the rollback step can undo it. DO NOT pass force=True unless the user has explicitly approved a validation bypass AND you supply force_reason — the response will carry validation_bypassed=true so the override is auditable.

failure_policy.notes: If apply returned ok=false with validation_passed=false, fix content and retry; do not silently flip force=True.

steps.title: Undo the apply when something downstream fails
steps.purpose: Restores the pre-patch snapshot. Run only if subsequent tests / type checks regress, or the user rejects the change. patch.v2 will refuse rollback for sessions flagged unsafe_legacy_session=true (pre-r7 path-traversal artefacts) — never blanket-rollback a session list without inspecting that flag.

failure_policy.notes: If rollback fails (already_rolled_back or unsafe_legacy_session) ask the user to inspect omni_patch(action='sessions') and the git diff manually.

success_criteria: post-apply omni_diagnostics returns no new errors on the modified file (compared to the pre-edit baseline from the diagnostics step)
suggested_tests or targeted commands from omni_impact are run, or explicitly deferred with a reason
memory_advisory action_items have been reviewed (or skipped with a reason)
If force=True was used, validation_bypassed=true is captured AND force_reason explains why
next_actions: Start with omni_context(file='${file}', symbol='${symbol}', task='${task}', token_budget=4000, format='json') to anchor the workflow
Then omni_impact(symbol='${symbol}', depth=2, format='json') to read the blast radius
Run omni_memory(action='advisory', symbol='${symbol}', task='${task}', format='json') and review action_items before drafting content
After applying, omni_diagnostics(file='${file}', severity='all', format='json') to confirm no regressions
Optionally omni_memory(action='store', category='solution' or 'mistake', symbol='${symbol}', content='<one-line lesson>') to record what worked
